dutiyachiggaḷayugasuttaṁ.meaning: the Second Chiggaḷayuga Sutta, the Second Discourse on the Yoke with a Hole

dutiyamasāmaññasuttaṁ.meaning: the Second Sāmañña Sutta, the Second Discourse on the State of the Recluse
breakdown: The state of a recluse, true asceticism.

dutiyaparisuddhasuttaṁ.meaning: the Second Parisuddha Sutta, the Second Discourse on the All-Round Pure

dutiyapaṭipadāsuttaṁ.meaning: the Second Paṭipadā Sutta, the Second Discourse on the Way of Practice
breakdown: Way of practice, path, mode of progress.

dutiyasaṁgīti, mahāvaṁsapāḷi..meaning: the Second Council (Dutiyasaṁgīti), as recorded in the Mahāvaṁsa Pāli (the Great Chronicle)
breakdown: The second communal recitation, the Second Council (dutiya, second + saṁgīti, chanting together).
The Mahāvaṁsa, the Great Chronicle of Sri Lanka, in its Pāli text.

dutiyo.gram: ordinal numeral of dvi (two), masculine nominative singular

duvidhakoṭṭhāse.meaning: divided into two parts; in a twofold division
duvidhakoṭṭhāse.gram: compound noun, locative singular or accusative plural

duṭṭho.meaning: full of ill will; corrupted by hatred
duṭṭho.gram: past participle of dussati (√dus), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Root meaning to spoil, offend, hate.
Past participle suffix: dus + ta > duṭṭha, spoilt, malevolent.

dvayaṁdvayasamāpattimethunappaṭisevanā.meaning: engaging in sexual intercourse, the coupling of two and two
breakdown: Two by two, in pairs (dvaya, a pair, doubled).
Practising, indulging in (the doubled pp is sandhi doubling).

dvayaṁdvayasamāpattiṁ.meaning: the union of couples; sexual intercourse entered upon two by two
dvayaṁdvayasamāpattiṁ.gram: feminine abstract compound noun, accusative singular

dvedhāvitakkasuttaṁ.meaning: the Dvedhāvitakka Sutta, the Discourse on the Two Kinds of Thought
breakdown: In two, twofold; divided into two.
These (masculine nominative plural). Sandhi: dve + ime > dveme.

dvinnaṁ.gram: cardinal numeral, genitive/dative plural of dve

dvādasākāraṁ.meaning: having twelve modes; the twelvefold aspect
breakdown: Mode, aspect, making. Sandhi: dvādasa + ākāra > dvādasākāra.

dāliddiyaṁ.gram: neuter abstract noun, nominative/accusative singular, from dalidda
breakdown: Abstract noun suffix: the state of being poor.
Enjoying, delighting in (past participle of ramati).

dāni, idāni.gram: indeclinable adverb (dāni is the shortened form of idāni)

dānāhaṁ.meaning: from now on I; now I

dārabharaṇāyā.meaning: for the support of a wife; for maintaining a family
breakdown: Dative singular ending: for the sake of.

dāsidāsapaṭiggahaṇā.meaning: (abstaining) from accepting female and male slaves
dāsidāsapaṭiggahaṇā.gram: neuter compound action noun, ablative singular

dātā.gram: masculine agent noun (dātar), nominative singular, from dadāti (√dā)
breakdown: Agent noun suffix: the one who gives.
Taking, receiving (ā + √dā): the one who takes the inheritance.

dīgha nikāya,.meaning: the Dīgha Nikāya, the Collection of Long Discourses
dīgha nikāya,.gram: proper noun; name of a canonical collection
dīgha nikāya,.goenka: The Dīgha Nikāya is the Collection of Long Discourses of the Sutta Piṭaka. A shorter version of the passage referred to in the source, recited also as a paritta (protective) chanting, is recited on day one during a regular 10-day course by S. N. Goenka.

dīghamaddhānaṁ.meaning: for a long period; a long stretch of time

dīgharattaṁ.gram: neuter compound noun used adverbially, accusative singular
breakdown: Night; by extension a span of time: for a long time.

dīghā.gram: adjective, plural or feminine form of dīgha

dīghāyukasaṁvattanikā.gram: compound adjective, plural or feminine form
breakdown: Having a life(span). Sandhi: dīgha + āyuka > dīghāyuka.

dūrangama.meaning: going far; far-ranging (said of the mind)

dūrato.gram: adjective with ablative suffix -to, used adverbially

dūratova.meaning: from afar indeed; even from a distance
dūratova.gram: adverb + emphatic particle, fused by sandhi

dūraṅgamā.gram: compound adjective, plural or feminine form

dūteyyapahiṇagamanānuyogā.meaning: (abstaining) from the practice of going on errands and carrying messages
breakdown: Carrying messages, the office of a messenger (from dūta, messenger).
Practice, application; ablative: from the practice of. Sandhi: gamana + anuyoga > gamanānuyoga.
Hello! hey! (exclamation of address). Sandhi: ehi + ambho > ehambho.

ehipassiko.meaning: inviting one to come and see (a quality of the Dhamma)
breakdown: Adjective suffix: of the come-and-see kind.
ehipassiko.goenka: Ehipassiko is one of the qualities of the Dhamma recollected in the traditional formula: the Dhamma invites everyone to come and see — to verify it directly for oneself rather than accept it on faith. The teaching is open to direct experiential examination by each meditator.

breakdown: Hole, opening (the doubled cch is sandhi doubling).

ekadesato.gram: masculine compound noun with ablative suffix -to
breakdown: Ablative suffix: from, by way of.

ekadivasaṁ.meaning: one day; on a certain day

ekaggacittā.meaning: with one-pointed mind; of well-directed, unified mind
ekaggacittā.gram: compound adjective, plural or feminine form
breakdown: One-pointed (eka, one + agga, point/top; the doubled gg is sandhi doubling).
Mind: having the mind gathered to a single point.

ekamantaṁ.meaning: to one side; at a respectful distance

ekantaakusalacittasamuṭṭhānattā.meaning: because of originating exclusively from an unwholesome mind
ekantaakusalacittasamuṭṭhānattā.gram: compound abstract noun, ablative singular (because of)
breakdown: Abstract suffix in ablative sense: because of being so.

ekapuggalavaggo.meaning: the Ekapuggala Vagga, the Chapter on the One Person

ekaputtamanurakkhe.gram: verb phrase fused by sandhi; accusative compound + 3rd person singular optative
breakdown: Would protect, guard (optative of anurakkhati: anu + √rakkh).
